places.name: Mumbai
places.description: Financial capital of India with a stunning coastline, vibrant nightlife, and a blend of colonial and modern architecture.
places.transport: Well-connected by air (CSIA), trains (CST, LTT, Dadar), and buses. Local trains are the lifeline of the city.
places.tips: Avoid June to September (heavy monsoons). The city is safe at night but always use registered cabs.

places.name: Delhi
places.description: The capital of India, a city of contrasts where Mughal monuments stand beside ultramodern malls. Packed with history and culture.
places.transport: Excellent metro connectivity. Well-connected by air (IGI Airport), rail (NDLS, NZM), and road.
places.tips: Summers (April-June) are extremely hot. Winters (Dec-Jan) can have fog that disrupts flights and trains.

places.name: Jaipur
places.description: The Pink City of India, famous for its stunning palaces, forts, and vibrant bazaars. A must-visit on the Golden Triangle circuit.
places.transport: Connected by air (JAI airport), rail, and road. Tuk-tuks and taxis are popular within the city.
places.tips: Summers are brutally hot (45°C+). Negotiate prices at local bazaars. Carry water when exploring forts.

places.name: Agra
places.description: Home to the iconic Taj Mahal, one of the Seven Wonders of the World. A city of unparalleled Mughal heritage.
places.transport: Connected by rail (AGC), Yamuna Expressway from Delhi. Tonga and e-rickshaws near monuments.
places.tips: Visit Taj Mahal at sunrise for the best photos. Avoid Fridays (Taj is closed). Book tickets online to skip queues.

places.name: Goa
places.description: India's party capital and beach haven. Known for pristine beaches, Portuguese architecture, spice plantations, and a relaxed vibe.
places.transport: Connected by air (GOI), rail (Madgaon/Thivim), and road. Rent a scooter for exploring beaches.
places.tips: Monsoon (June-September) brings lush greenery but rough seas and some beach shacks close. North Goa is party-focused, South Goa is quieter.

places.name: Varanasi
places.description: One of the oldest living cities in the world and the spiritual heart of India. Situated on the banks of the Ganges River.
places.transport: Connected by air (VNS), rail (BSB/MGS). Auto-rickshaws and boats are the primary local transport.
places.tips: Early morning boat ride on the Ganges is a must. Respectful attire required at temples. Book accommodation early during festivals like Diwali.

places.name: Bangalore
places.description: The Silicon Valley of India. A cosmopolitan tech hub known for its pleasant year-round climate, parks, and thriving food and pub scene.
places.transport: Connected by air (KIA), Namma Metro is expanding. Cab services are widely available.
places.tips: Traffic is heavy; use metro or cabs. Weather is pleasant year-round. Tech parks are concentrated in the east and north.

places.name: Hyderabad
places.description: The City of Nizams. Famous for its biryani, stunning Islamic architecture, pearls, and a booming IT sector.
places.transport: Connected by air (RGIA), MMTS rail, and expanding metro. Cab services are widely available.
places.tips: Buy pearls only from certified shops at Laad Bazaar. Visit Golconda Fort for the evening light and sound show.

places.name: Chennai
places.description: The cultural capital of South India. Known for classical dance, Carnatic music, ancient temples, Marina Beach, and auto industry.
places.transport: Connected by air (MAA), suburban rail, and expanding metro. Auto-rickshaws are plentiful.
places.tips: Summers (March-June) are very hot and humid. The northeast monsoon (Oct-Dec) brings heavy rains. Dress conservatively at temples.

places.name: Kolkata
places.description: The City of Joy. The cultural, intellectual, and artistic capital of India with colonial grandeur and incredible street food.
places.transport: Connected by air (CCU), Kolkata Metro (oldest in India), trains (Howrah/Sealdah), and iconic yellow taxis.
places.tips: Durga Puja (October) is a spectacular festival — the city transforms completely. Avoid summers (very hot and humid).

places.name: Udaipur
places.description: The City of Lakes. One of the most romantic cities in India with gorgeous lake palaces, intricate havelis, and Rajput culture.
places.transport: Connected by air (UDR), rail. Auto-rickshaws and taxis within the city.
places.tips: Hire a boat on Lake Pichola at sunset. The city gets very hot in summer. Book rooftop restaurants in advance for lake views.

places.name: Kochi
places.description: The gateway to Kerala. A cosmopolitan port city with a fascinating blend of Chinese, Portuguese, Dutch, and British heritage.
places.transport: Connected by air (COK), rail (Ernakulam), and road. Ferry is a popular and scenic local transport.
places.tips: Avoid the monsoon if you want beaches. The backwaters are best explored by houseboat. Kathakali performances are a must-see.

places.name: Shimla
places.description: The Queen of Hills and former summer capital of British India. Charming colonial architecture, lush pine forests, and mountain views.
places.best_time: March to June and September to November
places.transport: Connected by rail (Kalka-Shimla Toy Train, UNESCO Heritage), road from Delhi (~360 km). No flights to Shimla town.
places.tips: Snow in December-February makes it magical but chains/4WD needed. Mall Road is vehicle-free — walk around freely.

places.name: Darjeeling
places.description: The Queen of the Hills in the eastern Himalayas. World-famous for its tea gardens, views of Kangchenjunga, and Toy Train.
places.best_time: March to May and September to November
places.transport: Nearest airport is Bagdogra (NJP). New Jalpaiguri by rail, then shared jeeps or Toy Train to Darjeeling.
places.tips: Monsoon (June-August) brings landslides. Book Tiger Hill jeep the evening before. Layer clothing — mornings are cold.

places.name: Manali
places.description: A high-altitude Himalayan resort town, the gateway to Lahaul-Spiti and Leh-Ladakh. Popular for skiing, trekking, and paragliding.
places.best_time: March to June and October to November
places.transport: Connected by road from Delhi (~550 km). Nearest airport is Bhuntar (Kullu). Road can close due to snow.
places.tips: Rohtang Pass requires a permit (book online). Carry warm clothes even in June. Acclimatise before heading to Leh.

places.name: Amritsar
places.description: The spiritual heart of Sikhism, home to the magnificent Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib). Also the site of Jallianwala Bagh.
places.transport: Connected by air (ATQ), rail. Auto-rickshaws and cycle-rickshaws within the city.
places.tips: Golden Temple is open 24/7 and is free. Cover your head and remove shoes before entering. Wagah Border ceremony is at sunset.

places.name: Mysuru
places.description: The City of Palaces. Known for its magnificent Mysore Palace, jasmine flowers, silk sarees, yoga traditions, and the grand Dasara festival.
places.transport: Connected by rail (MYS), road from Bangalore (~145 km). No commercial airport.
places.tips: Visit during Dasara (October) when the palace is lit with 100,000 bulbs. Book Brindavan Gardens visit for the evening musical fountain.

places.name: Rishikesh
places.description: The Yoga Capital of the World and the Adventure Capital of India. Situated on the banks of the Ganges, a gateway to the Himalayas.
places.best_time: September to November and February to May
places.transport: Nearest airport is Jolly Grant (Dehradun). Connected by rail (Haridwar, 25 km). Regular buses from Delhi.
places.tips: Non-vegetarian food and alcohol are banned. Wear life jackets for rafting. The Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat is mesmerising.

places.name: Jaisalmer
places.description: The Golden City rising from the Thar Desert. Known for its stunning sandstone fort, sand dunes, and camel safaris.
places.transport: Connected by air (JSA), rail (JU). Jeeps and taxis are the main local transport.
places.tips: Nights in the desert can be cold even in winter. Book overnight desert camp in advance. Avoid May-August (extreme heat, 45°C+).

places.name: Puri
places.description: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites, famous for the Jagannath Temple, Rath Yatra festival, and beautiful beaches.
places.transport: Connected by air (Bhubaneswar, 60 km), rail (PURI station). Taxis and auto-rickshaws locally.
places.tips: Non-Hindus cannot enter Jagannath Temple. Visit Konark Sun Temple, a UNESCO site, on a day trip. Rath Yatra (June/July) draws millions.
